MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ROBERT SCHARFFER, KATHRYN BENSON, JOHN NEFF, TOM MATTLEY, AARON OBSTFELD, SHAWN THOMAS, ED NEWTON, DANIEL SERPA, ANDREW STANLEY, TERRY WATSON, ROSS JEROZAL, MICHELLE FLORES, RYAN ORR, MARK MELVIN, ERIK POSTON, JAIME ROCHA, EDWIN ROSARIO, MONTE THYDEN, NATHAN SPEAR, GREG SPYDEL, DAVID GUZZETTA, GREG MERRIMAN, AUDIO VISUAL DESIGN GROUP INC, INSPERITY PEO SERVICES LP, AUDIO VISUAL DESIGN GROUP-SAN JOSE LP, COOK-SCHARFER HOLDINGS INC, COOK-SCHARFER HOLDINGS LP'S MOTION TO TRANSFER is denied. The motion is untimely. Code of Civil Procedure 396b imposes mandatory time requirements for bringing this type of motion. (Barquis v. Merchants Collection Association (1972) 7 Cal. 3d 94, 115 ("A party's failure to raise a timely objection to venue has traditionally been considered a waiver of any 'right' to a proper venue. Section 396b of the Code of Civil Procedure represents an explicit codification of the general 'waivability' of venue defects, providing that, notwithstanding the governing venue provisions, an action may generally be tried 'in the court where commenced' unless the defendant makes a timely motion for change of venue") (internal citations omitted); Weil & Brown book at 3:564 (The motion "must be made within the time permitted to plead")). The five cases cited in the reply brief for the proposition that this court is empowered to excuse the untimeliness of the motion are distinguishable. One of the cases, Battaglia, does not address the timeliness issue and in all of the other cases a motion was filed within the time requirements of section 396b and the questions addressed by the courts were whether the timely-filed motions were sufficient and/or whether subsequent acts or events precluded a finding that later papers or motions were timely. In this case, defendants do not point to any motion or other act that took place within the time requirements of section 396b to render their motion timely. Essentially defendants contend that there should be an equitable exception to section 396b, but cite no authority for such an exception. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
